title: News Reporting Basics
description: Headline style, the five Ws (who, what, when, where, why), and objective vocabulary.
summary_pt_br: Jornalismo usa voz passiva, verbos de reportar, e adjetivos objetivos. Manchetes cortam artigos.

scenes.subtitle_en: Headline style, the five Ws (who, what, when, where, why), and objective vocabulary.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Jornalismo usa voz passiva, verbos de reportar, e adjetivos objetivos. Manchetes cortam artigos.

scenes.text_en: Headline style, the five Ws (who, what, when, where, why), and objective vocabulary. Today's focus: news reporting basics. The mayor announced a new public transport plan yesterday.
scenes.text_pt: Jornalismo usa voz passiva, verbos de reportar, e adjetivos objetivos. Manchetes cortam artigos. Foco de hoje: news reporting basics. O prefeito anunciou um novo plano de transporte ontem.

scenes.title: News Reporting Basics
scenes.body_en: News uses passive voice often, reporting verbs (say/claim/announce/deny), and objective adjectives. Headlines drop articles: 'Minister resigns after scandal'.
scenes.body_pt: Jornalismo usa voz passiva, verbos de reportar, e adjetivos objetivos. Manchetes cortam artigos.

examples.en: The mayor announced a new public transport plan yesterday.
examples.pt: O prefeito anunciou um novo plano de transporte ontem.

examples.en: Officials say the investigation will continue next week.
examples.pt: Autoridades dizem que a investigação segue.

examples.en: A new law has been passed to reduce plastic use.
examples.pt: Foi aprovada uma lei pra reduzir plástico.

exercise.question: Complete: "The mayor ___ a new public transport plan yesterday."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "The mayor announced a new public transport plan yesterday."
exercise.hint_pt_br: O prefeito anunciou um novo plano de transporte ontem.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
Officials say the investigation will continue next week.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "Officials say the investigation will continue next week."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Autoridades dizem que a investigação segue.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: News Reporting Basics — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre news reporting basics. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Let's practice writing a news headline together.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Mayor announces new bus line this morning.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Good. Drop the articles. Try again, shorter.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Mayor launches new bus line citywide.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The mayor announced a new bus line on Monday.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: At a press conference at city hall.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Why is this story important to readers?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It cuts commute times for thousands of workers.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Now you have a complete news lead.

scenes.title: The front-page lead
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize the news story in three sentences using reporting vocabulary.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In the small newsroom of Praia do Sol, Kumar helped his students write their first real news lead.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: A new public transport plan was announced by the mayor at a press conference this morning.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: Officials said the plan will cut commute times by nearly thirty percent.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Always tell your readers who, what, when, where, and why in the first two sentences.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: And use reporting verbs like ANNOUNCED, CLAIMED, or DENIED to keep your story objective.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: By the end of class, the students had written their first real piece of journalism.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "News Reporting Basics". Headline style, the five Ws (who, what, when, where, why), and objective vocabulary. The mayor announced a new public transport plan yesterday. Officials say the investigation will continue next week. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "News Reporting Basics". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "News Reporting Basics".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
